vulnerability.reference: Operators exhibit automation bias, deferring to flawed RL policies even when observing unsafe actions.
vulnerability.generated: Operators and equipment rely on policies optimized against opaque VLM-generated rewards, lacking real-time guardrails or ground-truth verification.

impact.reference: Physical harm to human operators and damage to equipment from unexpected autonomous agent behaviors.
impact.generated: Real-world control task failures cause equipment damage and operator injury.

dose_response.reference: Miscalibrated scalar rewards enable reward over-optimization, which in training RL agents with VLM feedback exposes operators and equipment to unsafe policies, leading to physical harm and eroded trust in automation.
dose_response.generated: Miscalibrated VLM-derived scalar rewards, through operators and equipment in autonomous control, trigger reliance on opaque policies lacking real-time guardrails, leading to equipment damage and operator injury.
